title: VimFu — zb Scroll to Bottom
description: zb scrolls the screen so the cursor line is at the bottom. See everything above your current position.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

setup.content:
# Tower Defense
# Small example for the VimFu zb demo.
import math
import random


class Enemy:
    def __init__(self, x, y, hp):
        self.x = x
        self.y = y
        self.hp = hp
        self.alive = True

    def take_damage(self, amount):
        self.hp -= amount
        if self.hp <= 0:
            self.alive = False
        return self.alive

    def step(self, dx, dy):
        self.x += dx
        self.y += dy


class Tower:
    def __init__(self, x, y):
        self.x = x
        self.y = y
        self.range = 5
        self.damage = 10
        self.cooldown = 1.0
        self.level = 1

    def upgrade(self):
        self.level += 1
        self.damage *= 1.5
        self.range += 1

    def in_range(self, enemy):
        dx = self.x - enemy.x
        dy = self.y - enemy.y
        dist = math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy)
        return dist <= self.range

    def fire(self, enemy):
        if self.in_range(enemy):
            enemy.hp -= self.damage
            return True
        return False

steps.say: Z B scrolls so your cursor line is at the bottom of the screen. See everything above. Great for reviewing what you just wrote.

steps.comment: --- Demo: scroll past init, jump back, zb ---

steps.say: Jump to the end of the file first.
Now back to the upgrade method on line thirty-four. The init method is way off the top of the screen.
Cursor at the bottom. The whole Tower class above it is visible --- including the init method I wanted to check.
Z Z centers. Z T scrolls to top. Z B scrolls to bottom. Three scroll commands, three perspectives.
Z B. Cursor to the bottom. Use it when context is above you.
